CD8, CD4, CD3, and other key co-receptors and adhesion molecules develop as part of mature T-cell function. However, among these choices, B (PAX5), C (CD34), and D (TdT) have been shown to be important during early hematopoiesis or T cell precursor stages rather than being consistently present throughout all phases of mature T cells. The only option that is not typically found on fully developed T-lymphocytes but can transiently appear during their maturation from progenitors would be A (CD1a). Therefore,
